name: Azosemide
description: Azosemide is a loop diuretic used to treat hypertension, edema, and ascites [1,2].
mechanismOfAction: Exact mechanism of action is unclear. However, it acts primarily on the loop of Henle, in both the medullary and cortical segments of the thick ascending limb. [1]
pharmacodynamics: Diuretic affects upon oral administration match those of furosemide. However, upon intravenous administration azosemide displays 5.5-8 times greater effect.

interactions.name: Mecamylamine
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Azosemide is combined with Mecamylamine.

interactions.name: Sodium phosphate
interactions.description: Azosemide may increase the nephrotoxic activities of Sodium phosphate.

interactions.name: Candesartan cilexetil
interactions.description: The risk or severity of hypotension can be increased when Candesartan cilexetil is combined with Azosemide.

interactions.name: Acemetacin
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Azosemide can be decreased when used in combination with Acemetacin.

interactions.name: Tramadol
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Tramadol is combined with Azosemide.
